candidate_segments.approx_location_description: Southern edge of Neuss, southern part of the Rosellen district. Primarily low-rise residential areas south of the A57.
reason_for_selection: High degree of morphology consistency with Norf pilot
Predominantly detached and semi-detached housing stock
Official zoning as decentralized heating zone (Prüfgebiet Einzellösungen)
No known major district heating infrastructure

candidate_segments.approx_location_description: Compact suburban residential district south-west of the city center, surrounded by agricultural land.
reason_for_selection: Homogeneous residential structure with high roof-to-building ratio
Structurally isolated from central district heating nodes
High potential for individual heat pump adoption and roof PV
Stable suburban property values supporting investment

candidate_segments.approx_location_description: Residential clusters on the western periphery of Holzheim, extending towards the agricultural fringe.
reason_for_selection: Significant share of modern or fully renovated residential units
Large roof areas suitable for commercial-scale residential PV and storage
Geographically distant from the primary SWN district heating network
Good accessibility for field survey and truth-grounding

candidate_segments.approx_location_description: Specific residential blocks on the outskirts of Allerheiligen that are not connected to the legacy 1999 Fernwärme network.
reason_for_selection: Leverages high-quality 90s/2000s building stock where DH is absent
Excellent architectural suitability for air-source heat pumps
Possibility of 'truth-bridge' adjacency to Norf segment
Requires careful spatial masking to avoid DH-covered core
